800101.title: 백설선택지1
800101.prevDesc:
The vines growing on the ground are a hassle.
Maybe there’ll be a chance to get rid of them?
800101.eventDesc:
A heart torn into thousands of pieces must be kept from all eyes, thus it burrowed into the ground and tangled itself.

Yet, someone has to pull up these thorny roots,
as there is no wound that can remain unearthed.

The Sinners gave each other looks.
Being coiled in piercing, sharp pain was something to avoid even in one’s dreams.
800101.behaveDesc:
The dry earth must be upturned.
Which Sinner was up to the task?
successDesc:
{0} walked with shaky steps.
With bare hands, they dug up the roots.

In their hands were the doubts of others.
The Sinner realized…

That while the injury may heal, distrust might take deep root in their heart.
failureDesc:
{0} could not uproot anything.
It was no easy task, after all—entangled so tightly together, one couldn’t make heads or tails of it.

Clutching itself, the durable web of fiber continued to expand,
and the Sinners will continue to suffer.

800102.title: 백설선택지2
800102.eventDesc:
The ground began to tremor ever so slightly.
The Sinners remained quiet, but they could read from each other’s glances the single thought they shared:
“Shouldn’t one of us step up to endure the pain so that the rest will be spared from suffering?”
800102.behaveDesc:
A small crack was found in the ground.
Who will be the one to seal it?
successDesc:
The root impales {0}’s chest and extends skyward.
The Sinner gasps for air, but that’s none of your business.
failureDesc:
The roots whisper as they skewer each Sinner:
“All of us should feel the pain.”
“Just as your sadness throws gloom over me, so should pain be felt equally.”
The thousands of roots, a unified whole, piercing into everyone’s flesh.

800104.title: 백설선택지4
800104.eventDesc:
As if to demonstrate the unrewarding harshness of life, and in spite of enduring so much pain, not a single flower seemed to bloom from the corpse.
Instead, the Sinners discover fruits borne on it.

One audibly swallows to drain their salivations.
An urge to know the taste of a fruit made fertile with a Sinner starts to grow.
800104.behaveDesc:
A Sinner eventually reached for the fruit.
Who among them took the bite?
successDesc:
{0} took a bite out of the fruit.
Their tongue is doused in pure delight.

Yet it’s more of a curse, as no other flavor will ever again be able to rouse them.
failureDesc:
Something was eating away at the Sinner from the inside.
It was something more humiliating than a bug craving sweetness, no; it was an emotion, an ignoble feeling.

The Sinner looked around.
They were now envious of everything, no longer able to love themself.

800105.title: 백설선택지5
800105.prevDesc:
The fruit was borne again.
Maybe this is a chance to destroy it once and for all?
800105.eventDesc:
The Sinners peered at its face.
They gazed upon the weathered memories within…

Down to the holes left by pests.

An apple does not culminate when it ripens to a glossy red.
When the apple browns, cracks, shrivels, and attracts lowly creatures…
That is when it has met its golden bliss.

The Sinners knew now was the time to bring the apple to the earth.
800105.behaveDesc:
The apple is open for attack.
Who was the Sinner to bring down the apple in its moment of bliss?
successDesc:
{0} brought the apple to the ground.
The fall left a dull thud.

The fruit was emptied of its lifeblood long ago; only futility remained.
This attack allowed the Sinners a brief moment of respite.
failureDesc:
{0} could not strike the apple.
It must have been the Sinner’s wavering heart that caused them to falter.

The mind is such a superficial thing.
In any mind, you will find a sense of inferiority that likes to belittle the self.

800801.title: 구더기사과선택지1
800801.prevDesc: Maybe the Golden Bough lies inside the apple?
800801.eventDesc:
The apple’s body broke, and the raging fruit still grew.
Could the Golden Bough really be in there?

“I’ll go check it out!”
Yuri said to the others.

The Sinner with a bug’s arm seems nervous about this.
Does he want to go with her, perhaps?
800801.behaveDesc:
You can’t just send Yuri out there alone.
What Sinner will be able to keep her safe?
successDesc:
The fruit’s cracked shell suddenly began to rattle as if it would jump at any moment.

{0} managed to escape, but it seems Yuri couldn’t.
The fruit’s flesh melted to form a new body.

Don’t feel too guilty about it. One lost life is better than two.
failureDesc:
The fruit’s cracked shell suddenly began to rattle as if it would jump at any moment.

{0} was too occupied to notice it in time.
They managed to get away but ended up injured.

Yuri was even later to notice. The fruit’s flesh had already melted with her to form a new body.

It’s unfortunate, but there’s nothing we can do about it. One of our own got hurt, too.

800201.prevDesc:
The Abnormality looks upset.
It seems to want something, but what should we give?

800202.title: 아즈텍원판선택지2
800202.eventDesc: It looks like one of the Sinners will have to do a clay doll’s job.
successDesc:
It appears that {0}’s blood was fresh enough.
Satiated, the Abnormality calmed down for a moment.
failureDesc:
It didn’t seem to like {0}’s blood.
The Abnormality looks even madder than before.

800203.prevDesc:
The heat of the Abnormality is wearing everyone down.
We’ll have to make an offering to calm its wrath.

800204.title: 아즈텍원판선택지4
800204.eventDesc: It looks like one of the Sinners will have to do a clay doll’s job.
800204.behaveDesc: Whose heart would be the coldest among them?
successDesc:
{0} was able to cool the Abnormality’s fervor.
The flame shrunk to a flicker.
failureDesc:
Maybe {0} was warmer than we thought.
The Abnormality’s belching an even fiercer flame now.

800205.behaveDesc:
The stars are twinkling.
When they shine, they’ll rob us all of our sight.
What gift would the stars like?

800206.title: 아즈텍원판선택지6
800206.eventDesc: It looks like one of the Sinners will have to do a clay doll’s job.
800206.behaveDesc: Whose voice could lull the stars to sleep?
successDesc:
{0}’s lullaby brought the stars into a deep slumber.
Their starlight will leave us be for a while.
failureDesc:
{0}’s lullaby ended up waking the stars.
It appears that they are about to punish us.

An unimaginably bright flash will burn all of our eyes.
Perhaps the cosmos will reveal itself before our scorched sockets.

801201.prevDesc: You defeated the enemy, but now it’s back up. What’s going on?

801501.prevDesc: Strips of pink ribbons float this way.

801502.title: 분홍신선택지2
801502.prevDesc:
“Snap out of it! Whatever it’s telling you, it’s all lies!”

Someone shouted.

Thanks to that warning, you realized that baubles of greed adorned the ribbons.
You have to untie the desire-laden knot.
801502.eventDesc: The knot of desires must be undone.
successDesc:
“Aargh…”

“My head is pounding.”

“Nnh… Was just getting good.”

The ribbons binding the team scattered and melted away.

“Freedom! Freeness!”

An unfamiliar voice reaches your ears, but for now, you should help the Sinners recover first.
failureDesc:
The pink ribbons ended up enshrouding everyone.
You can’t find the strength to resist, or even think.

It seems…

You’ll be forever unable to escape.

10100201.title: Eternal Rest
10100201.eventDesc:
A soldier was crushed under rubble, gasping in agony.

“Please just kill me… It hurts too much.”

You can’t ignore this person.
10100201.behaveDesc: Who will undertake bringing this soldier to peace?
successDesc:
“Thank… you…”

The soldier’s life was delivered a coup de grâce by the knife.
The human rhythm that rose through the blade soon ceased, and the soldier was eased into eternal rest.
failureDesc:
“Gyaagh…!”

The knife came down upon the wrong spot.
The soldier’s bloody coughs and face full of terror felt like they endured for an eternity.

It took a while for that suffering soldier to pass away.
Regret and hate were the final things in those eyes.

10200201.title: Exhilarating Edutainment Featuring Excess Violence!
10200201.eventDesc:
“Liking red color! Liking red color! Like it! Like it!”

A group of cyborgs yelled along to a strange rhythm.

“Red 4!!!”

Exulting the number high, they spun the roulette.
Then they handed a roulette ball to one of the Sinners.
10200201.behaveDesc:
It seems the ball has to enter a red pocket with a number higher than 4.
Who should spin the ball?
successDesc:
“Losing! Defeat!! Urgh-ack!”

The “upgraded” croupier fell to the ground, buzzing and sparking.
Even its final throes felt rhythmic.
failureDesc:
“Get beaten! Get all thrashed, real pulped!”

As soon as the cyborg saw the ball roll into the wrong pocket, it swung its massive arm to smack the Sinner.

With the Sinner crumpled on the floor, the game had to come to a stop…

10200202.title: Exhilarating Edutainment Featuring Excess Violence!
10200202.eventDesc:
“No time now~ To stay all~ H. U. R. T!”

The chuckling cyborgs went back to the roulette.

“Like yellow color! Like yellow color! Yellow! Yellow! Yellow!”

“Yellow 6!!!”
10200202.behaveDesc:
This time, the ball has to enter a yellow pocket with a number higher than 6.
Who should spin the ball?
successDesc:
“All lost! Foil again!! Ya-aaa-argh!”

The “upgraded” croupier fell to the ground, buzzing and sparking.
failureDesc:
“Here comes the fist to you~! Ready? Bready-tready-steady!”

The cyborg swung its massive arm to smack the Sinner.

With the Sinner crumpled on the floor, the game had to come to a stop…

10200203.title: Exhilarating Edutainment Featuring Excess Violence!
10200203.eventDesc:
“Can’t say no to fun! Not. One. Little tiny bit!”

The singing group of cyborgs spins the wheel.

“Liking blue color! Liking blue color! Bluey! Bluey! Bluey!”

“Blue 8!!!”
10200203.behaveDesc:
This time, the ball has to enter a blue pocket with a number higher than 8.
Who should spin the ball?
successDesc:
“Everything! Gone up bust!! Dammit!”

The “upgraded” croupier fell to the ground, buzzing and sparking.

All the other cyborgs broke down with it at the same time.
Have we won…?

You noticed something glowing in their pile of tin rubble.
You carefully picked it up.
failureDesc:
BAP!

The cyborg swung its massive arm to smack the Sinner.

“GAME OVER! Consolation prize time!”
The group of cyborgs tossed something at us, then walked away.

1030201.title: Before a Sealed Door
1030201.eventDesc:
The steel is thick, but it might not be impossible for a Sinner to move it ajar.
Who would be the right person to do that?
successDesc:
{0} pushed at the door with all their might.

“Hnngh…”

The door shook little by little but refused to give way.

“All right, you’ve done enough…”

I tried to get the Sinner to stop, but {0} kept on pushing, fully focused on the door.

After some time…

“There!”

The door was opened.
A path was hidden behind it.
failureDesc:
{0} pushed at the door with all their might.

“…It won’t work.”

The door didn’t so much as budge.

As much as you wanted to know what was behind that door…
There was no choice but to leave the room for now.

1030202.title: Before a Sealed Door
1030202.eventDesc:
There’s got to be some way through other than brute force.
Perhaps a thorough look around could reveal something.
1030202.behaveDesc: An attentive Sinner would be good at this…
successDesc:
{0} looked around the door.

“……”

After tinkering with something on the wall,
{0} let out a triumphant grunt.

“Huh?!”

The door was open.

Apparently, there was a protruding brick next to the door.
{0} explained that sliding it back into place got the door to open, then proceeded through.

There was a hidden path behind the door.
failureDesc:
{0} looked around the door.

But, they were unable to find anything even after having a good crack at it.

“Oh well…”

We had no choice but to leave the room behind.

1030301.title: The First Gate
1030301.eventDesc:
The forest is dense, but there are still gaps within it.

If we tread carefully, we might be able to strike the Inquisitors from behind.
It’d give us a considerable advantage in the upcoming battle.
1030301.behaveDesc: Who’s best suited for sneaking about the forest for an ambush?
successDesc:
{0} slowly walked into the woods.
Soon enough, a few Inquisitors fell to the ground.

“Heretics! The heretics are here!”

Though we couldn’t take them all down at once, it was enough to break their formation.
We’ll be able to pass through once we take the rest down.
failureDesc:
{0} slowly walked into the woods.
Soon enough, a commotion broke out at the gate.

“What is it?”

“Looks to be a heretic; what are they doing here?”

“Must’ve tripped.”

…Sounds like the ambush was unsuccessful.

1030302.title: The First Gate
1030302.eventDesc:
We might be able to distract them by making some noise.
The problem is how to go about it…

“Hear ye, hear ye—for I have a plan!”

Might as well hear her out.

…You’re not sure if it’s the best idea exactly, but it might not hurt, either.
1030302.behaveDesc: Then, who will be in charge of putting it into action?
successDesc:
{0} made their move.

“Wait, is that…”
“Hey, we should check it out.”

The Inquisitors guarding the gate left their post to investigate the source of the noise.

“So doth the diversion return! ‘Tis exactly as I spake! A plan without flaw, from conception to conclusion!”

The mastermind proudly exclaims.

Honestly didn’t expect it to work this well…
We’ll be able to pass once we’ve dealt with the remaining Inquisitors.
failureDesc:
{0} made their move.

“…A heretic! Throw the nails!”

“They were coming from that direction, saw a dozen other heretics there.”

…Of course this would happen.
No choice but to fight them head-on now.

1030501.title: Who Judges the Judge
1030501.eventDesc:
“Heretics! The heretics have appeared!”

As the Sinners approached, the Inquisitors cast their victims aside to charge at us.

“You bastards!”

Sinclair stood against them at the forefront.
But, that hammer might be too big for Sinclair’s weapon to stop.
1030501.behaveDesc:
Should Sinclair really deal with it?
Or, should you order another Sinner to repel the attack?
successDesc:
You figured it’d be best to ask {0} to do it.
The hammer bounced off, and the Inquisitors were perturbed by the retaliation.

We’ll be able to keep the flow of the battle in our favor by taking the lead now.

After all, this is his fight first and foremost.

“Raaaah!!”

Sinclair locked his halberd against the handle of the hammer and parried it to the side.

With their leader’s attack thwarted, the Inquisitors were thrown into disarray.
We’ll be able to keep the flow of the battle in our favor by taking the lead now.
failureDesc:
You asked {0} to deflect the attack, but…
The motion of the giant hammer couldn’t be stopped.

The Inquisitors surrounded {0}, who was now on the back foot.
We have to fight on with this disadvantage.

1030601.title: A Rotting Corpse’s Memento
1030601.eventDesc:
A Sinner easily enticed by trinkets and baubles would probably be willing to dig through this morbid mess to take the item.
On the other hand, someone uninterested in such things will not be happy to do this order.
1030601.behaveDesc: Who should go in and retrieve the item?
successDesc:
{0} found a bent sigil of N Corp. between the manes.

It might not have been worth reaching into a corpse for, but it has to prove useful somewhere down the line.

You took the sigil and left the room, comforting the Sinners who were retching at the sight.
failureDesc:
{0} rifled and dug through the manes, but ultimately found nothing.

“Hrk… Geh…”

The more the corpse was ransacked, the more {0} retched.
…This won’t be worth pursuing further.

Telling {0} that they did their best, we left the room.
We lost something and gained nothing.

{0} rifled and dug through the manes, but ultimately found nothing.

“Hrk… Geh…”

The more the corpse was ransacked, the more {0} retched.
…This won’t be worth pursuing further.

Telling {0} that they did their best, we left the room.
We lost something and gained nothing.

1030801.title: Rite of Suppuration
1030801.eventDesc:
“…It won’t work. Too many Inquisitors are guarding the other route.”

We had a Sinner scout the perimeter, and it looks like this is the path of least resistance.
Someone with a burning will might be able to bear this pain.
1030801.behaveDesc: Who should be sent to tread the path?
successDesc:
{0} ran across the heated road on bare feet.
The others frowned at the rancid stench of burning flesh.

Groaning with agony, {0} made it to the other side…

An altar with a red seal and a lever rose before them.
When {0} pulled the lever, the road quickly cooled.
The rest of us were able to tread the path safely.
failureDesc:
{0} ran across the heated road on bare feet.
The others frowned at the rancid stench of burning flesh.

{0} almost made that painful advance midway…

But they couldn’t bear it any longer and hurried back.
It looks like we’re not taking this route.
We’ll have to follow the other path and deal with the many Inquisitors there.

Don Quixote declares:

'I shan't walk bare of feet!'

1031001.title: Rite of Confrontation
1031001.eventDesc:
It appears that sitting on the chair is how you begin the rite.
What kind of rite it may be is unclear… but it’s the only way to open the gate.
1031001.behaveDesc: What Sinner will be made to sit?
successDesc:
{0} sat on the chair.
…Dreadful screams filled the room.
A constant stream of tears ran down {0}’s face.

Soon enough, the bowl was full of tears.
When it was placed on the altar at the door, the altar slid down, opening things up.

“Gasp, huff… Hic.”

{0}’s eyes were shaking.
It’s probably best not to ask what they saw.

“…There’s a sigil beyond the door.”

Taking the blue sigil, we left the room.
failureDesc:
{0} sat on the chair.
…Dreadful screams filled the room.
A constant stream of tears ran down {0}’s face.

But it wasn’t enough to fill the bowl.
It seems the Sinner was so shocked that tears struggled to even wring themselves out.
We left the room, helping {0}, stricken with sorrow, to their feet.

1031201.title: Rite of Penance
1031201.eventDesc: Someone must step on the spikes and impale their own limbs.
1031201.behaveDesc: Who should be sent to the spikes?
successDesc:
{0} stood on the spike-laden device.
You quietly nodded when the Sinner looked back with trembling eyes.

…A terrible scream echoed through the hall.

Soon enough, the door rumbled open.
Inside, an orange sigil was waiting to be picked up.
We took the sigil and ushered the impaled {0} out of the hall.
failureDesc:
{0} stood on the spike-laden device.
You quietly nodded when the Sinner looked back with trembling eyes.

…A terrible scream echoed through the hall.

But there was no response.
Maybe the Sinner hadn’t suffered enough?
We made sure to have {0} try the rite several times, but the door still didn’t open.

1031401.title: Rite of Conclusion
1031401.eventDesc: There doesn’t seem to be any other way; conversation is our only option.
1031401.behaveDesc: Who’s the best speaker among us all?
successDesc:
{0} approached the Inquisitor.

“…You, well-spoken one. Your words, they were persuasive.”

After a while of parlance, the Inquisitor opened the gate for us.
failureDesc:
{0} approached the Inquisitor.

“……”

Yet the Inquisitor remains silent.

“…Rejected.”

We could only hear refusals from time to time.
{0} eventually had to give up on talking and returned to us.
…Looks like we’ll have to speak with our fists.

103003.title: 던전 103003 선택지
103003.eventDesc:
An inscription above the closed gate reads:

Prove your terror and fears
Fill this vessel with tears
103003.behaveDesc: Who among us can spill the most tears?
successDesc:
{0}’s tears filled the bowl.
The pedestal lowered into the ground, and the gate opened.
failureDesc:
{0} couldn’t shed enough to fill the bowl.
The door had to be pushed open with everyone’s combined strength.

103004.title: 던전 103004 선택지 : 짓무름의 의식
103004.eventDesc:
You see a door on the other side of a red-hot passage.
The sign next to it reads:

Prove your weakness and suppuration
Sear a path with mobilization
103004.behaveDesc: Who can cross this heated passage?
successDesc:
{0} walked across the burning road on bare feet.
When the Sinner made it to the other side, a panel rose next to the door.

When {0} used it, the passage cooled.
The rest of us were able to tread through safely.
failureDesc:
{0} set foot on the burning road with bare feet.
However, the Sinner turned around and came back, unable to bear the heat.
We all had to cross the searing passage together.

103005.title: 던전 103004 선택지 : 속죄의 의식
103005.eventDesc:
A passage is written above the large gate.

Only those who repent may find motion
Thorn yourself, atone through flagellation
103005.behaveDesc: Who would be best at repentance?
successDesc:
{0} stood on the spike-laden device.
A painful scream echoed through the hall.

Soon enough, the door rumbled open.
Looks like {0} has been rid of emotional baggage.
failureDesc:
{0} stood on the spike-laden device.
A scream echoed through the hall.
But there was no response.

We only got through after trying the rite several times.

10300601.title: 던전 103006 2차 선택지
10300601.eventDesc: Let’s try to talk things out first.
10300601.behaveDesc: Which Sinner would be able to sway the Inquisitors?
successDesc:
{0} approached an Inquisitor and delivered a captivating speech.
After some discussion, they opened the way for us.
failureDesc:
{0} approached an Inquisitor to try and hold some dialogue.
After a few short exchanges, the Inquisitor kicked out our Sinner.

Seems today the sword is mightier than the pen.

802501.title: 늑대선택지1
802501.prevDesc:
The wolf is crouching!
Is it preparing to pounce?
802501.eventDesc:
After getting in position, it leaps for us.
It’s approaching faster than expected.
802501.behaveDesc: Who might halt the flurried beast?
successDesc:
The swift beast was stopped in its tracks.
Now it glares at us in a wary stance.

It won’t stay that way for long, though.
Perhaps that’ll remind it of the pain that left those scars…
failureDesc:
The wolf’s advance and subsequent assault were unhindered.
Was there ever a chance to deter what’s too fast for the eyes?

The attack left a serious wound.

802801.title: 뱀선택지1
802801.prevDesc:
Something’s different about its aura!
Has its anger boiled over?
802801.eventDesc:
The rampage continues.
It might reach a critical point faster than expected.
802801.behaveDesc: Who will stop the monster’s wrath?
successDesc:
The Sinner delivered a powerful attack to the raging foe.
It seems to be thrown into confusion by this unexpected move.

This could be our chance.
Or a greater threat…
failureDesc:
An attempt to counter the monster was made.

However, it missed as expected, angering our foe further.

802901.title: 크로머선택지1
802901.prevDesc:
There’s something foreboding about Kromer’s gaze.
Her eyes are filled with hatred and yearning!
802901.eventDesc:
Kromer feigns the appearance of being calm and collected.
But the nail in her hand gleams like a poniard, waiting to tear through the facade of calm.
802901.behaveDesc: Who will take Kromer’s pointed implement head-on?
successDesc:
Kromer was struck by the unexpected counterattack.
Her hateful eyes lost focus for a moment.

The sharp weapon in her hands failed to pierce anyone.
It fell to the ground in tranquilizing silence.
failureDesc:
Kromer stumbled for a moment, but deftly dodged the counterattack.
She backs down as if she expected this and glares into our group like a hunter seeking prey.

Someone has to be the victim of that hatred and yearning.

803001.title: 크로머선택지2
803001.prevDesc: Kromer’s getting fatigued. She’ll expose a vulnerability soon.
803001.eventDesc:
Kromer is breathing heavily. Perhaps it’s a side effect of the transformation.

Her posture is unstable, and her eyes are looking elsewhere.

With her focus disrupted, we might be able to land a critical strike.
803001.behaveDesc: Who will be able to seize that opportunity?
successDesc:
{0} circled around Kromer’s fleshy mass to disorient her.

Soon enough, she let her guard down.
{0} didn’t miss this chance to deliver a devastating blow.

That seemed pretty effective. A deep, indelible wound was left on Kromer’s body.
Could this be it?
failureDesc:
{0} circled around Kromer’s fleshy mass to disorient her.

“…Ah.”

However, for some unknown reason, {0} froze stiff on the spot.

Was it the perturbation of witnessing her writhing flesh up close, or was it the fear that Kromer’s eyes struck?

The child couldn’t so much as try drawing their weapon before returning to your side. They seem mentally exhausted.

There’s little you can do about it. Just fight her the usual way.

803101.title: 단수어선택지1
803101.prevDesc: That headless thing’s belly seems to have stretched to its limit.
803101.eventDesc:
Its abdomen has filled with red liquid.
It looks like it might expel it at any moment.
A bubbling splash of blood can be seen around its severed neck.
803101.behaveDesc: Who could jam its throat shut?
successDesc:
{0} quickly ran over and looked up at its neck.
The Sinner had no means to seal a severed throat.
After a quick brainstorm, {0} used their weapon to cut its belly open.
Liquid spilled out onto the ground, losing all of its built-up pressure.
failureDesc:
{0} quickly ran over and cut its neck.
However, shortening an already sliced throat wasn’t going to weaken that bloody stream.

“A mistake.”

One Sinner commented, but what is done is done.
There was only one chance, and it has been squandered.

803201.title: 골목파수견선택지1
803201.prevDesc:
Thunderous roars gather and violet sparks grow restless.
Where should they go?
803201.eventDesc:
Purple flashes are jumping between the poles.
If they aren’t captured, something bad will happen.
803201.behaveDesc: Who will nab those pesky bolts?
successDesc:
{0} lodged their weapon into the ground.
“Did you fry your brains?”
Someone outcried, but it took little time for that concern to be proven wrong.
The bulky bolt of lightning gravitated toward the Sinner’s weapon and was sucked into the ground.
“…It will then be conducted.”
Indeed, the rejected sparks jumped into the utility pole near the weapon.
Ironically enough, the king of the alleyway was struck by electricity.
failureDesc:
{0} lodged their weapon into the ground.
The Sinner was trying to guide the rampaging sparks to the earth.
“…It’s too low. It won’t strike here.”
Indeed, the bulky bolt of lightning directly headed for the utility poles without hesitation.
“Oi, doesn’t that look… grim?”
Unfortunately, the king of the alleyway now had red lightning under its command.

851001.title: Blubbering Toad
851001.prevDesc: The toad seems to be taking a huge breath.
851001.eventDesc:
The toad had taken a deep breath, inflating its belly.
A blue teardrop or two welled in its huge, round eyes.
This cavern will be filled with an air of gloom once its mouth opens.
It would be best to knock the wind out of it.
851001.behaveDesc: Who will stop its cry from coming forth?
successDesc:
“I do worry that this might be a little cruel…”

{0} gut-punched the inflated vocal sac.

“Pfarhk!”

A deflating sound rang out in the cave.

“Croough…”

Shrunk back to a slim shape, the toad sniffled. It must have been frustrated that it couldn’t cry out loud like it wanted to.

“How gloomy.”

Though it might have left a bad taste… an opportunity is still an opportunity.
failureDesc:
{0} approached the toad.

“Croak!”

Perhaps the Sinner was a bit too careful.

When {0} got to the toad, its mouth had already begun to open, exhaling a staggeringly gloomy sound.

“Croohoo… Croohic… Croohoo…”

{0} lost consciousness, trapped in sorrow.
Now they merely cry, just as the toad does.

851901.title: Kqe-1j-23
851901.prevDesc: The mechanical arm is reaching for the ground. What is it up to?
851901.eventDesc:
The mechanical puppet stretched its wires onto the ground.
Those wires then dug underneath like roots.
Is it trying to get electricity from the floor?

The robot’s terminal printed out:

<Please Cooperate!>

“…Hold on, doesn’t that mean it can’t do anything while it’s charging?”

The Sinners seem rather unwilling to listen to its request.
851901.behaveDesc: Who can sabotage its attempts to charge up?
successDesc:
{0} moved quickly.
The mechanical puppet remained still—a perfect opportunity.
A clear sound soon rang in the area.
The Sinner delivered a powerful attack that shattered the puppet’s capacitor.

<Please Cooperrr… Csdk…ppra…@#@%!%^#$>

The terminal was filled with random letters shortly before turning off.
What a perfect strike, wasn’t it?
failureDesc:
{0} moved quickly.
The mechanical puppet remained still—a perfect opportunity.
But alas…

“W-Wait!”

Before the others could warn them, {0}’s weapon struck the wires that the puppet had plugged into the floor.
It should go without saying that touching a high-voltage current with anything remotely conductive has its consequences.
{0} was zapped stiff by the electricity.
Fortunately, the shock wasn’t deadly, but the machine began to move… now pulling its wires off the ground.
Such as it is.
Things don’t always go as planned, do they?

852701.title: My Form Empties
852701.prevDesc: The statue looks as tranquil as ever. What could it be thinking?
852701.eventDesc:
It feels as though you find peace in merely looking at it.
The statue seems to be embracing the agony and anguish of all.
There is no self within—only everyone.
It can only make sense that one would lose the will to fight.
However, no man can wholly shoulder another’s suffering in their stead.
Before we all are allured by its false compassion, someone has to break the statue.
852701.behaveDesc: Who will be able to break this deceitful statue?
successDesc:
{0} shook their head.
As they looked around, the Sinner saw that everyone else was bowing to the statue. Some were uttering odd mantras as well.
{0} realized that such tranquility was the result of brainwashing.
There is no need to describe how the worshippers would have felt upon realizing this truth.
A powerful attack shattered the statue, and everybody returned to their senses.
failureDesc:
{0} trudged their way to the statue, feet heavy like its stone.
No one could stop the Sinner, as all were busy lowering their heads and bowing to it.
{0} murmured some sort of mantra along the way.
Listening closely, it was a confession. The Sinner was uttering all the negative pasts and thoughts in their mind.
{0} felt that their heart grew lighter and forgiveness grew closer as they divulged, but it was not to come.
The fake bodhisattva was only biding its time to restore its strength, paying little attention to the confession.
And now…
Time is up.

91030201.title: Tango of Poultry Passion
91030201.eventDesc:
The chicken holding some stitching supplies pleads with chicky eyes.
Someone has to do this. Those eyes cannot be ignored.
91030201.behaveDesc: Who has the hands for this task?
successDesc:
{0} hesitantly stepped forward.
“Mmh…”
Taking the needle and thread, the Sinner sat behind the chicken.
With awkward but careful hands, they began to stitch the seam with a standard method.
“Kieph.”
Fortunately, it doesn’t seem to feel the pain of the needle.
Once the sewing was done, it hopped into {0}’s lap as if to thank them.
“Kieeh!”
{0} was pleasantly perplexed.
failureDesc:
{0} confidently stepped forward.
“Let’s see…”
Taking the needle and thread, the Sinner sat behind the chicken.
In spite of the initial confidence, however, their stitches became gradually wry.
“Kieerh!”
Before {0} could finish, the chicken pushed the Sinner away.
“…Why didn’t you do it yourself in the first place?”
It snatched the supplies back to sew itself up, then walked out of sight.
{0} was left perplexed.

804501.title: Shock Centipede
804501.prevDesc: The centipede writhes as if to coil up.
804501.eventDesc:
The Shock Centipede's body is starting to coil up into one spot.
"It seems electricity is gathering."
As the Sinner pointed out, sparks are flying from the center of its coiled body.
It might be a good idea to stop it.
804501.behaveDesc: Who will be able to stop it?
successDesc:
[{0}] struck at the Shock Centipede's carapace.
"Ohh...!"
A crack opened up in its hard-looking armor.
Could it really have stopped the centipede?
This ought to be a rare opportunity.
failureDesc:
[{0}] struck at the Shock Centipede's carapace.
Alas...
Could the stored power have sprung forth?
Tremendous showers of sparks popped out of the Centipede's center along with a thunderous boom.
Naturally, [{0}] was knocked away by the shockwave.
Fortunately enough, it only pushed the Sinner away and did little harm...
But the Centipede looks quite angry.
It'd be a good idea to brace up, right?

804601.title: Drifting Fox
804601.prevDesc: Could those ragged umbrellas protect that fox, really?
804601.eventDesc:
Old umbrellas are lined up around the Drifting Fox.
Old, worn, holed umbrellas.
The umbrellas look like they've been standing there for Wings know how long trying to protect the fox, but it certainly doesn't seem like they're doing a good job at it.
"……."
Then, a Sinner slowly walked toward it.
The fox noticed the Sinner approaching, but it doesn't growl, nor does it happily relax.
It stays still, keeping its ever-constant place.
successDesc:
[{0}] walked toward the fox before getting under a worn umbrella.
Despite being an umbrella, it allowed numerous drops of rain to fall through it.
Chilling winds and damp air brush against the Sinner under the umbrella.
"……."
But [{0}] raised their head up to see the sky, making a profound smile.
As if the Sinner knew the rain will stop someday.
failureDesc:
[{0}] walked toward the fox before getting under a worn umbrella.
Despite being an umbrella, it allowed numerous drops of rain to fall through it.
Chilling winds and damp air brush against the Sinner under the umbrella.
"…Haah."
[{0}] drooped down with a large sigh.
It may not be easy to notice from afar,
but it must have been the cumulation of gloom and dampening the person under the umbrella felt.

804701.title: So That No One Will Cry
804701.prevDesc:
The cursed wooden doll has stopped moving.
What could be going on?
804701.eventDesc:
The wooden doll stopped in place as if nothing was happening.
Its head was tilted slightly downward like it was examining its talismans...
Or perhaps it could have been looking at the Sinners' talismans.
"……."
As if possessed, the Sinners began to walk toward the wooden doll.
It was like they forgot they were fighting just moments ago.
And they started removing the talismans entangled on their bodies.
Just what could be going on?
804701.behaveDesc: Who will gather the talismans and return them?
successDesc:
[{0}] gathered the detached talismans and stood before the wooden doll.
It seems to be gazing silently.
Or, maybe it was just the Sinner's conscience making it seem that way.
The eagerness to return the talismans to where they were.
[{0}] extended their arm to hand over the stack of talismans...
As if drawn by a magnet, the talismans flew toward the doll.
"So that no one will cry..."
After muttering the phrase, [{0}] came back to their position.
failureDesc:
[{0}] gathered the detached talismans and stood before the wooden doll.
"……."
It creaked a little, then stretched a hand at [{0}].
Thinking it's to retrieve the talismans, [{0}] handed over the stack,
"Ah!"
The wooden hand changed its trajectory and touched the Sinner's body.
At that moment, all the talismans on [{0}] began writhing before melting into sticky blood.
…That doesn't bode well, does it?

804801.title: Fairy Gentleman
804801.prevDesc: An intoxicating smell fills the air, it could almost get you drunk.
804801.eventDesc:
The Fairy Gentleman spoke with a jolly laugh.
"Huhu... Didn't think this much would be gulped down."
A short hiccup could also be heard.
"I admit, it caught me off guard a tiny bit... Just a tiny bit."
The fairy then puts the cup in his hand to his mouth.
"Hey! Is that thing trying to sober up?"
"We must keep the blackguard disoriented!"
…It seems the fairy must be stopped from drinking from the cup.
804801.behaveDesc: Who will take the cup from the gentleman?
successDesc:
[{0}] stood before the gentleman's mountainous belly.
"Ah, I couldn't share this with you, even if you're a privileged guest."
Instead of demanding it, [{0}] just stares into the fairy.
"...Ahem."
Completely still, not moving a muscle.
"...A-Alright, alright! I can give you... a little bit."
[{0}] shook their head.
"Are you... telling me to give you all of it?"
The Sinner nodded.
"You..."
"Fuu, haha. Fine, I've lost. You can have this."
[{0}] finally made a light smile as they claimed the cup.
failureDesc:
[{0}] stood before the gentleman's mountainous belly.
"Ah, I couldn't share this with you, even if you're a privileged guest."
Instead of demanding it, [{0}] just stares into the fairy.
"...Ahem."
Completely still, not moving a muscle.
"...Standing like that won't change my answer, you know?"
After vigorous laughter, the fairy gulped down all of the cup's content into his stomach.
"As much as I care about you, I couldn't give you everything I have, could I? Haha."
Defeated, [{0}] returned.

1040101.title: Looking for a Drone
1040101.eventDesc: A Sinner good at searching should be able to help.
1040101.behaveDesc: Who will be the most efficient at the job?
successDesc:
Several wounds were left on [{0}]'s hands while rummaging through the wreckage.
Fortunately, we were able to find a small ampule in it.
"After going through that trouble..."
The other Sinners sighed at the ultimately pointless haul.
...We had to leave the room with my promise that I'll turn the clock for them next time.
failureDesc:
The broken debris was sharper than expected.
"…!"
Several wounds were left on [{0}]'s hands while rummaging through the wreckage.
"Guess there's nothing."
Nothing useful could be found after several more minutes of searching.
"……."
I feel the resentful glares of the Sinners on my back.

1040301.title: Poking at the Egg
1040301.eventDesc:
Considering our experiences with receiving gifts from Abnormalities,
we might just be able to get something to help with exploring the dungeon here.
1040301.behaveDesc:
Who will be the most effective at the job?
A delicate hand will have an easier time.
successDesc:
"Whoa!"
[{0}] touched the egg. It started glowing.
Soon afterwards, it hardened and turned into a uniquely shaped gem.
"This looks worth taking."
[{0}] seemed rather tired, but there wasn't anything off about the body as far as we could see.
We left the place with the gem in our possession.
failureDesc:
"Whoa!"
[{0}] touched the egg. It started glowing.
However, the egg soon crumbled into dust.
"Ugh..."
A mysterious wound was left on [{0}]'s hand.
And the Sinner seemed tired as well.
...We left the room, consoling [{0}].

1040401.title: PickingRowAndColumn
1040401.eventDesc:
When you look closer, you can almost see something else in there.
A Sinner with keen eyes might be able to get something good.
successDesc:
[{0}] looked at the cabinet.
The Sinner discovered a hidden drawer. The drawer contained a number of useful items.
Soon afterward, the area began to blur, and the cabinet disappeared.
failureDesc:
[{0}] looked at the cabinet.
Perhaps the Sinner spent too long pondering; the area began to blur, and the cabinet disappeared.

1040801.title: Try Talking
1040801.eventDesc: All the Sinners faced the window.
1040801.behaveDesc: Who will talk to the figures?
successDesc: Icriedwithoutachoice. Oh ▽ ! Iaminpain. Ientertainthethought: HowmustIcry. When [{0}] talked to the figures in the window, they writhed and disappeared. Then a fragment fell from the window. We took it and left the room.

Icriedwithoutachoice. Oh ▽ ! Iaminpain. Ientertainthethought: HowmustIcry. When [{0}] talked to the figures in the window, they writhed and disappeared. Then a fragment fell from the window. We took it and left the room.
failureDesc:
…………..
[{0}] tried talking, but there was no response.
Soon enough, the figures writhed and disappeared.
"What, did they want us to sing or something?"
We left the room with the Sinners complaining.

805002.title: Stand in his way.
successDesc:
[{0}] managed to block Dongrang's attack.

"Haah... You're being a nuisance."

Dongrang muttered as his weapon bounced off.
Beneath his bitter chuckle was a cold fury.
failureDesc:
[{0}] tried to stop Dongrang, but alas...

"Haha, what a futile thing you're doing."

Perhaps the attack was more powerful than anticipated. The Sinner was the one that got knocked away.

"Haah... You're being a nuisance."
"I'd much prefer if you served as seeds... for my accomplishment."

Beneath his mocking grin was a cold fury.

805004.title: Stand in his way.
successDesc:
[{0}] drove their weapon into Dongrang while his focus was elsewhere.

"Kgh..."

It looks like the attack was successful.
The move Dongrang was preparing didn't come through.

"I see where you stand..."

"Then I'll just get rid of you all along with Yi Sang and his ideal."
failureDesc:
{0}] drove their weapon into Dongrang while his focus was elsewhere.

"Such incompetence."

However, what seemed to be the gap in his defense was merely his trap.
The Sinner was knocked away by his attack.

"Hah, I see where you stand..."

"You leave me with no choice. I'll get rid of you all along with Yi Sang and his ideal."

805006.title: Stand in his way.
805006.eventDesc:
"……."

Yi Sang walked ahead.

"I must insist... that I mark the end for Dongrang."

"...For that would be most ideal."
successDesc:
Yi Sang managed to stop Dongrang in his path.

"Yi Sang..."

"I plead you, end this now... Dongrang."

"Haha... How can I?"

"In the end, someone... has to... cry tears, isn't that right...?"

Dongrang pulled the weapon lodged into himself deeper inside.

"…!"

"Let's end it this way... Yi Sang..."

805901.title: Fairy Longlegs
805901.prevDesc: The sky seems to have been covered again. Why does it keep casting that shade?
805901.eventDesc:
The giant plant that the fairy made grow was as thick as it was tall.

It did have its perks.

Like hiding behind a building's column, one Sinner managed to escape the fairy's view.

The Sinner then carefully struck at the stem.
successDesc:
Striking at the stem, [{0}] climbed up the plant, hand over foot over hand.

The Sinner climbed so high, the others and the fairy appeared as tiny dots.

The plant seemed endlessly tall, but it had its end.

At last, [{0}] climbed onto the massive leaf at the top.

Then the Sinner carefully walked up to the edge...

And poked a hole in the leaf, causing the pool of water to fall on the fairy's head.

The fairy is making quite a fuss, looking surprised.
failureDesc:
Striking at the stem, [{0}] climbed up the plant, hand over foot over hand.

The Sinner climbed so high, the others and the fairy appeared as tiny dots.

The plant seemed endlessly tall, but it had its end.

At last, [{0}] climbed onto the massive leaf at the top.

But perhaps the Sinner failed to consider the amount of water that ponded on the leaf...

As soon as [{0}] stepped on the leaf, they slipped and fell right to the ground.

...It might be fortunate that the Sinner didn't die.

successDesc:
The whirling spiral slows down,
showing forgiveness.

Reverence for a being of no significance
is followed by a slight self-contempt.

The spiral descended out of sight in the meantime.
failureDesc:
Though you turned away, it keeps pulling you in.
As if drawn by a magnet,
all eyes are pulled toward it.

The spiral has not forgiven.
Contempt for your hesitation to show the courage not to face it takes over.

You feel the encroaching spiral slowly dig its way in.

successDesc:
We carefully walked into the dense fog.
Eerie howls surround us.

It was no longer possible to tell which way was forward.
All we could do was keep walking.

The fog cleared at one point,
and a trinket of endless ashen flow awaited us.

We then realized it wasn’t fog we walked through.
failureDesc:
We carefully walked into the dense fog.
Eerie howls surround us.

It was no longer possible to tell which way was forward.
All we could do was keep walking.
After a while, even advancing began to feel terrifying.

The fog grows thicker.
It feels as though you will no longer be able to escape.

successDesc:
You stared into the eye-like things.
Your piercing gaze met the song
of endless despair and wailing sockets.

You then realized that it wasn’t fog you were facing.
What you thought were its eyes and mouth
all turned out to be tricks played by shadows.

When you figured out that its howl was merely whistling wind,
all of it vanished.
failureDesc:
You stared into the eye-like things.
It returned the gaze, singing endless despair.

Just as you felt that the figure seemed to have grown,
we were engulfed by the fog.

successDesc:
With each rope that’s untied, the wail grows louder.

You feel sympathy for the trapped being.

Undoing the ropes, you begin to weep
along with their wailing.

One undone knot accompanies sorrow.
One undone knot accompanies lamentation.

When the last is released,
you discover that there is no one inside.

Perplexed, you examine the interior,
and find a single, red-jeweled beetle.
failureDesc:
With each rope that’s untied, the wail grows louder.

There’s more than one individual occupying the coffin.
Several people are clinging to each other, all crying.

The coffin bursts open, letting them out.

90100502.behaveDesc: Let’s just go back. Who should lead the way?
successDesc:
The red arms wave in the air,
as if pleading not to be left behind.

But, after another look,
it also seems as if they’re thankful for being left as they are.

Though the wailing hasn’t ceased,
the weeping seems to have softened, just a little.
failureDesc:
You disregard the chilling sensation and move on.

However, that feeling would linger,
even after a while of walking.

At one point, you stopped and turned around
to find a hand.

It was attached to an unnaturally scrawny red arm.

It grabbed its speechless victim.
A familiar note was added to the coffin’s wailing.

successDesc:
{0} picks up a knife from the ground and slides it across their hand.

The flesh splits open, and dark blood gushes out of the wound onto the coffin.

Red fluid trickles along the bound rope...

The rattling coffin eventually comes to a slow stop, and the wailings fade.
failureDesc:
{0} picks up a knife from the ground and slides it across their hand.

The flesh splits open, and dark blood gushes out of the wound onto the coffin.

Red fluid trickles along the bound rope...

The coffin begins to shake. Something scratches at its walls.

It sounds as though something is stepping across the soil; the knot dances in the wind.

A dessiccated arm emerges from the coffin and grabs onto {0}.

Yet, the undone knot and the dessiccated arm soon fades into nothingness like mirages.

successDesc:
“You kent that?”

The paper flapped in disbelief.

“Well, if you really insist, knowing what’s coming…”

It took a step back, dousing itself with coffee.
Just then, a paper soldier
suddenly rushed at you.

“You shouldn’t underestimate paper’s integrity.”

Before you could respond to the peculiar voice,
the soldier attacked you.
failureDesc:
“You sure you read it right?”

“With a body like that, you can’t fulfill the contract written here!”

The paper sprayed coffee on the floor,
spewing insults.

Before we could make any excuses,
we got kicked out.

successDesc:
“‘Preciate it. You made the job easier.”

The paper soldiers nearby
start fighting each other.

Sometime later, the defeated soldiers’ bodies are torn,
exploding into paper cranes.

One of the cranes crawls your way.

“Take good care of that piece.”

The satisfied sheet of paper drenched itself in coffee.
failureDesc:
“That your way of turning someone down?”

Annoyed, the paper crumpled.

“There are basics to proper conversation.
I don’t wish to speak with someone
who can’t respect those basics.”

Before we could make any excuses,
we got kicked out.

successDesc:
You removed the nails from the teddy bear.

Bloody pus pours out of the holes.

Once the plush had finished its copious exudation,
only a dark cavity remained within.

It is an empty void.
failureDesc:
The nails plunged into its chest were buried deep.

Nothing would budge even after several tugs and pulls.
It’s all firmly lodged in the cotton base.

The plush looks down at it
as if nothing is happening.

Perhaps it’s unable to feel pain.

successDesc:
You picked up the ring in its hand.
It remains perfectly still, maintaining its chant.

This statue won’t move,
no matter what happens around it.

Though it stays forever still,
and the tone of its mantra remains consistent…

You knew one thing.

Its chants are imbued with a curse.
failureDesc:
You tried to pick up the ring in its hand.
Its wrist suddenly spun in a circle.

You reach for the ring again,
but that wrist keeps moving, denying you.

It could be conveying that you are unworthy.

successDesc:
Its growl recedes.

You stroke it once more,
and it closes its eyes, pleased.

You stroke it once more,
and it settles on the ground, comforted.

You stroke it once more,
and it shrinks to become a statue.
failureDesc:
When you reach out to pet it,
it growls louder.

When your finger touched its bristled fur,
it jumped, yelping violently.

You try to approach it as it gives you a fierce glare,
but it runs off without turning back.

What else should you have done?

successDesc:
When you detached one,
a candle in the room suddenly began to burn.

With another detached,
the fire burns even brighter.

The wooden doll begins
to tremble ever so slightly.

Is it right to continue?

Is this what it wishes for?
failureDesc:
Nothing happened.
The doll would simply stand there, producing small noises.

The talismans in hand have turned into sticky blood,
slipping between your fingers.

successDesc:
It’s a tablet of unknown letters.

While you’re unable to fully grasp it,
a few words stand out.

Judgement…
Retribution…
Punishment…

You also see some combinations of words
that might have deadly consequences if you try to understand them.

When you look back up, the scribe gives a short nod
before soaring into the sky.
failureDesc:
It’s a tablet of unknown letters.

You feel you might be able to understand it.
It just needs a little more effort.

A little more… Just a little more…

When you come to your senses, the being has disappeared.
You were left alone with the tablet in your hands.

successDesc:
You pick a rose out of it.

With closer examination, you notice
that it has an intestinal texture.

What is a flower-shaped organ for?
failureDesc:
You pick a rose out of it.

Your careless removal
has crushed the uniquely textured flower.

The sign shows no change.

successDesc:
You contain some of the slime in a glass jar.

This isn’t simply a mass of filth.

Numerous hatchlings swim inside.

These tiny creatures are feeding on each other;
could they be the clam’s offspring?
failureDesc:
You approach with a glass jar in hand.

However, hesitation takes hold, and you eventually turn around.

You couldn’t stand the thought
of reaching out to contain that filth.

In fact, you get the feeling that it’s something you mustn’t hold.

successDesc:
As you head for the giant flower,
the smaller ones make way.

They close up their petals as you pass,
as if to bow down in respect.

Once you arrived at the end,
the giant flower drooped low to offer a present.
failureDesc:
You head for the giant flower.

The small blooming ones scuttle and shuffle.

They seem to be staring from all directions.
You feel like you might get lost.

Looking back up,
the giant flower still waves to you.

You couldn’t approach the flower no matter what you tried.

All you could do was gaze at it from a distance.

A sudden wave of nostalgia prompts you to wave.

In an instant, all the flowers went awash with red,
simultaneously turning to face you.

After staring into us for a little while,
their red petals flew at us.

It was like a punishment for making a half-hearted gesture.

successDesc:
As soon as it’s shown where to go,
it runs in that direction.

You notice it dropped something as it began to sprint.

A blue lighter with a Wing’s logo on it.

You heard stories of that Wing
providing its employees with supplies like this.

It would seem that it was an employee of the transport company, not a passenger.
failureDesc:
While you were in contemplation,
it slowly shambled your way.

When you point in a random direction out of panic,
it changes course to follow the instruction.

Moments later,
you heard the noise of metal plates crumpling, and something else ripping apart.

successDesc:
{0} takes a few steps, and the passenger follows.

As we draw closer to what appears to be an exit, the passenger bows down as though to show us gratitude.

We heard something fall; it left behind what appears to be a patrol flashlight.

Looks like it wasn't lost after all. It may have been an employee of a transport company that happened to be patrolling the area.

Maybe we didn't guide it—maybe it was merely following us to make sure that we found the right exit.
failureDesc:
{0} takes a few steps, and the passenger follows.

After a while, {0} accidentally leads us back to where we started.

The passenger stands there, somewhat confused and awkwardly waving its arms, before striding forth and beckoning us to follow.

It led us to the exit.

successDesc:
You quickly ducked out of its path.

Among the onslaught of the obsessively encroaching hands is a particularly small hand.

You reach out and pull on that hand. The statue ceases to move.

In your hands is an unforeseen, symmetrical figurine.
failureDesc:
You quickly ducked out of its path.

However, it was no easy task
to avoid its obsessive hands.

A few of them managed to grab and scratch at us.

Though we managed to escape, we suffered a lot.

successDesc:
It lifts a nearby object
to carry it from left to right.

The count on its body went up by 1.

Just as you started to wonder if that was it,
the machine replaced one of its vacuum tubes with a new one.

It presented the old one to you,
and naturally, you accepted.
failureDesc:
It lifts a nearby object
to carry it from right to left.

While it was moving the object,
it suddenly came to a halt.

The vacuum tubes in its body suddenly flicker.

After flashing random letters and numbers,
everything shut off.

With its power gone, the object slipped from its hands and fell.
…A few of us were standing in the impact zone.

successDesc:
“Apply stimulation and pain to the centipede
to increase the discharge intensity.”

So writes a message above the buttons.

When you pressed the drop-shaped button, the tank was filled with water.

The centipede twists its body as if in some sort of dance.

It didn’t seem to be out of joy.
failureDesc:
“Apply stimulation and pain to the centipede
to increase the discharge intensity.”

So writes a message above the buttons.

When you pressed the drop-shaped button, the tank was filled with water.

The centipede twists its body as if in some sort of dance.

You watched the centipede’s tail scratch the tank’s surface. 
The glass cracked and shattered to pieces immediately afterward.

The electrified water cascaded all over us.

successDesc:
"Aha, good! Here, have a drink."

It feels as though a hard, burning liquid is rampaging around the body.

"Mmm, I like the way you drink it!"

A steadily floating sensation is followed by the feeling that you're in better shape somehow.

"Feeling the magic at work? This is what we call medicinal booze! Go on then, go for another shot!"

After a few more shots of the liquor, we left the place with gleeful steps.
failureDesc:
"Aha, good! Here, have a drink."

It feels as though a hard, burning liquid is rampaging around the body.

"Hey... You alright?"

A sudden nausea is followed by the view slowly turning to the right.

"A real shame... It's such a good drink... but I guess your body doesn't agree with it. You should stop drinking it."

Trying to hold the eyes and head that were starting to spin faster, we left in a hurry.

The way out might have been accompanied by bumps and slams into walls, but even that blurred out of memory.

successDesc:
"Oho! Then here, have a drink."

{0} began chugging the strong liquor.
It's uncertain whether {0} really likes to drink, or is simply trying to indulge the entity.

"Hot damn, looks like the hooch's finally found its match!"
"Y'know what's better than neckin' the whole bottle?"

Something chucks a huge barrel of liquor toward us from beyond the thick miasma of steam.
We barely managed to catch the barrel. A lively voice booms in our direction.

"A keg stand!"
failureDesc:
"Oho! Then here, have a drink."

{0} began chugging the strong liquor.
It's uncertain whether {0} really likes to drink, or is simply trying to indulge the entity.

"Hot damn, looks like the hooch's finally found its match!"
"Y'know what's better than neckin' the whole bottle?"

Something chucks a huge barrel of liquor toward us from beyond the steam.
The abruptness of that aggressive throw completely caught us off guard; without a soft landing spot, the barrel shatters against the ground.

"A keg sta— Aw, what the heck! Y'know how precious that barrel was?!"
"Bah! At least take the barrel splinters with you. It's got a pretty strong, top-notch aroma of its own."

successDesc:
The laughing skull was lifted.

Elated for whatever reason, it keeps clattering its teeth and wobbling on the hands.

All of a sudden, you feel your body getting lighter.

Looking around, you notice that everyone seems to be experiencing the same thing.

When you looked back at the hands, the skull disappeared before you could notice.
failureDesc:
The laughing skull was lifted.

Strangely enough, the skull's expression suddenly turned blank as soon as it was held.

Wondering what might be the problem, the Sinner moved their head closer to the skull, and...

"?!"

The skull opened its jaws wide and bit {0} before disappearing.

successDesc:
The angry-looking skull was lifted.

Upset for whatever reason, it clattered its teeth as if it was trying to bite something in the air.

All of a sudden, the hands felt lighter.

"Is this..."

The skull vanished into thin air, and a glowing object was left on the hands.
failureDesc:
The angry-looking skull was lifted.

Strangely enough, the skull's expression suddenly turned blank as soon as it was held.

Wondering what might be the problem, the Sinner moved their head closer to the skull, and...

"?!"

The skull opened its jaws wide and bit {0} before disappearing.

successDesc:
The skull with a bizarre expression was lifted.

As if driven crazy, it rolled around and clattered its teeth without any rhyme or reason to it.

Just as you started to be unsure of what to do with it, the hands felt lighter, so you looked down...

And found that it turned into a tiny trinket.
failureDesc:
The skull with a bizarre expression was lifted.

Strangely enough, the skull's expression suddenly turned blank as soon as it was held.

Wondering what might be the problem, the Sinner moved their head closer to the skull, and...

"?!"

The skull opened its jaws wide and bit {0} before disappearing.

successDesc:
"How do I comfort a droplet..."

As odd as the order sounds, the Sinner does their best to lift its spirits.

The droplet seems to have perked up thanks to the Sinner's efforts.

"I could be free thanks to you."

It feels as though the droplet said something like that.

Soon enough, the droplet disappeared from view, and an object that looked like a big glass container was left in its place.
failureDesc:
"How do I comfort a droplet..."

As odd as the order sounds, the Sinner does their best to lift its spirits.

Alas... it didn't seem to help the droplet shake off its gloomy mood.

"Thanks anyways, this is for your trouble."

It feels as though the droplet said something like that.

The droplet handed us half of itself, and disappeared in the blink of an eye.

Seeing as the Sinners are saying they're feeling refreshed, its half must have been a gift.

successDesc:
It's only right to put it back where it belongs.

The droplet is lifted and pushed into the giant glass container.

And we watched.

We watched the face of the droplet being filled with terror, contempt, anger, and loathing as it slid down the tube.

"You shouldn't have done that."

"We might have looked like one in your eyes. But to me, this is hell."

It felt like it told us such things.

Along with the glass container, we could hear something within our hearts crack.
failureDesc:
It's only right to put it back where it belongs.

The droplet is lifted and pushed into the giant glass container.

And we watched.

We watched the face of the droplet being filled with terror, contempt, anger, and loathing as it slid down the tube.

"...Ahh!"

While we were distracted by the face, the droplet bounced off and slid out of view.

...Maybe it was something we shouldn't have done.

successDesc:
We told the person that the things in their hands are just blue marbles.

"Is that so. That's how you see them."

The person remained tranquil as if they knew you'd tell them that.

"If you want to look at them that way, then I say go ahead."

They disappeared, gently waving the marbles.

It feels like we've learned an important truth.
failureDesc:
We told the person that the things in their hands are just blue marbles.

"You sound pretty confident. Do you know what a star is in the first place?"

"Speckles of light floating in the dark. That's what we call stars."

They gently wave the marbles in their hands.

"Stars are what's in your mind. So these blue marbles are stars."

As the upright manner of speech was about to be etched on the heart, they disappeared, telling us they'll 'go to the blue star'.

It feels like we've learned an important truth.

successDesc:
Seeing the numerous hues of light in them, they ought to be stars for sure.

"I want to return to the blue star someday."

Could that be where they come from? Will I be able to go there?

"Do you think so too? That you want to return to that place."

You nod. You have no idea where it may be, and you certainly aren't from it, but you feel the urge to go back to it.

"Then I'll give this to you."

They handed over a blue star they were holding and disappeared.
failureDesc:
Seeing the numerous hues of light in them, they ought to be stars for sure.

"Right, but..."

Their tone suddenly changes to a melancholic one.

"I know. That I can't go back to it anymore."

"That I'm just waving my arms and emitting the blue glow to become a star myself."

With a whistling noise, it vanished into thin air.

Our heads somehow felt lighter after this.

successDesc:
It was focused on its prayers.

Doing your best to ignore the rotting stench, you manage to take a prayer book off its body.

It's so deeply immersed that it doesn't notice us approach it and take the booklet at all.

Perhaps its faith was too deep for prayer books to hold any meaning.

Whatever beliefs and doctrines it follows, they would already be firmly rooted in its mind and heart.
failureDesc:
It was focused on its prayers.

Doing your best to ignore the rotting stench, you manage to take a prayer book off its body.

"Gieeegh?!"

Was our attempt noticed? As soon as the booklet was removed, an unpleasant noise bursts out.

We braced ourselves for possible attacks, but it didn't seem to care about us.

It scraped the ground with its mandible, writing something down.

...It seemed to be the content of the prayer book.

successDesc:
While it stood still, we carefully approached it.

If we remove a part of it, those bound to it might find momentary peace.

"...!"

Taking off a piece of the wire was surprisingly easy.

However, the flesh lumps show no change at all.

It's as though they are telling us that this won't be remotely sufficient to free them from what they suffered that day.
failureDesc:
While it stood still, we carefully approached it.

If we remove a part of it, those bound to it might find momentary peace.

"...!"

We weren't familiar enough with the wire's structure. The hand twists and gets dragged into the wire in an instant.

"G-Get it out, quickly!"

The arm was pulled out thanks to the help of several people, but it was left severely mangled.

successDesc:
Was it by pity or some kind of sympathy?

Or was it the idea that sharing the pain might reduce their burden?

Drops of blood fall from the tip of the hand and seep into the flesh, little by little.

The thing finally responds, approaching as if to look down on you.

Maybe it saw me as the same as those who perished in the thick fog.

When it opened its mouth with a pitiful look, a glowing object was inside.

It might be a small token of consolation it's offering. You took the object.
failureDesc:
Was it by pity or some kind of sympathy?

Or was it the idea that sharing the pain might reduce their burden?

Drops of blood fall from the tip of the hand and seep into the flesh, little by little.

"...Urk!"

All corpses and bodies bound to the wire scream at once.

The thing begins to rampage in unison.

As I was not part of the war nor I was there to witness it, I cannot associate with or understand them in any way.

It was merely a fit of hasty arrogance.

There was no choice but to flee from the place, leaving the raging thing behind.

successDesc:
The O-ring snaps rather easily. It might have been weakened by age.

"Kff, kurgh..."

As Enkephalin pours out of the box, we face the person bloated and drenched with the substance through the glass window on the box.

Knowing that it is no longer human, sympathy causes a hand to be offered.

"Put it away."

It spoke.

"This was the only choice I had. To survive..."

"We were buried here with no hopes of rescue. We've been abandoned."

"I... We shoved our heads into Enkephalin so that we can endure the time."

The explanation is then followed by resentment.

"Why are you trying to save us after all this time? I want nothing more than to dip my head into that green water right now!"

Its face was full of anger, it ran off with the empty box still on its head.

All that it left was a small employee card and the guilt of doing what shouldn't have been done.
failureDesc:
Was the ring sturdier than it seemed, or was it the lack of skill?

The blade kept bouncing off the slippery O-ring...

"Brgrrgh..."

And the submerged thing pushed you away and ran off.

Did it prefer to stay like that?

All it left was a small employee card.

successDesc:
"Ah..."

You moved your leg to the side and pointed below, to the trembling apple's dismay.

"Hah, got you."

"No! I don't want this!"

"Stories must follow their natural courses. This is the flow we shall follow, so accept it."

It picked up the tiny apple and put it on the back of the horse.

"This won't be needed anymore. It's not the most flattering gift, but I will give it to you as a reward for letting me know."
failureDesc:
Just as you thought you'd tell the truth, you felt a tremor at your leg.

"...You were hiding it."

You try to deny it, but it was already angered.

"Get lost!"

It lashes at you viciously with a thorny snare...

And when you managed to dodge the onslaught, you found that the snare caught the rotten apple.

"This is the natural course. Accept it."

Having caught the apple, it ran off in the other direction.

successDesc:
When I point to some random, incorrect direction, the entity approaches and looms over me.
In place of its head was but an eyeless rose, but... I could still feel its piercing gaze.

"Such tale is your wish? Then I hope you have it within you to be responsible for your own words."
"Should you come across the apple in your journey, I bid you to bind it with this lasso."

The entity tossed a lasso at us before gallopping off astride the great steed of vines.
Only when could we no longer hear the sound of galloping hooves did the apple slowly emerge from behind my leg, shuddering.

"C-can you please get rid of that lasso? Please... I'm scared."

The apple pleaded, but some Sinners disagreed.
The lasso may come in handy.
I denied the apple's request, wound the lasso on my arm, and continued onward.
failureDesc:
When I point to some random, incorrect direction, the entity approaches and looms over me.
In place of its head was but an eyeless rose, but... I could still feel its piercing gaze.

"Such tale is your wish? Then I hope you have it within you to be responsible for your own words."
"Should you come across the apple in your journey, I bid you to bind it with this lasso."

The last sentence must've scared the apple—it flinches.
The entity noticed the hem of my pants shake slightly and erupts into a furious bellow, swinging the lasso.

"BEGONE!"

The apple tries to hurry away from the entity, but it's soon snared by the barbed lasso.
After fastening the apple to the vine horse's leg, the entity gallops into the forest.
The apple's desperate cries for help slowly fades away from us.

successDesc:
I command death to it.

"...Your command is understood. A great sense of purpose was detected in your voice."

"The command shall be carried out."

It disappeared after the short reply.

Soon enough, a loud noise rang from a distance.

...Then, the body felt lighter and invigorated.
failureDesc:
I command death to it.

"...Your command is understood. However-"

"I do not detect any sense of purpose in your voice. You are speaking without knowing why the command is to be made."

"You will be punished for giving meaningless commands."

Before a word could be said, the hand on its tail comes forth.

successDesc:
I command life to it.

"...Your command is understood. A great sense of purpose was detected in your voice."

"The command shall be carried out."

It disappeared after the short reply.

Soon enough, brisk steps could be heard.

As the footsteps went over the head, a shining thing fell from above.

...Could this be their thanks?
failureDesc:
I command life to it.

"...Your command is understood. However-"

"I do not detect any sense of purpose in your voice. You are speaking without knowing why the command is to be made."

"You will be punished for giving meaningless commands."

It took away the glowing things that had been collected.

successDesc:
I did not give it any command.

"None can go without giving any commands."

Watching its tail sway menacingly, we hastily waved our hands and gave an excuse.

"...Your command is understood. I now see what you meant."

Could something we said have elicited a response?

"The command shall be carried out."

It placed a mysterious object before leaving.
failureDesc:
I did not give it any command.

"None can go without giving any commands."

Watching its tail sway menacingly, we hastily waved our hands and gave an excuse.

"You have additionally committed the crime of neglecting to command even yourself."

"The crime of neglecting that obligation will be punished rightly."

The improvised excuse didn't work, and the tail was swung our way in a large arc.

successDesc:
Did you place it with sincerity?

Then the wish may come true.
failureDesc:
To place your wish on top of countless others...

The hand needed to be lighter and more cautious.

One stone falls to the ground, followed by many others.

The price for ruining the tower won't be light.

90105102.behaveDesc: Who will bring down the cairn?
successDesc:
You see something buried beneath the collapsed cairn.

We may never know why it had to be buried discreetly.
failureDesc:
As the stones collapsed in such a short time,

You attempted to avoid them to no avail, being pressed under their weight.

Now you were the one trapped in the stone cairn.

successDesc:
Declining the offer, we pressed on in the rain.

While running, you noticed something in your hand.

Perhaps it's the serendipity of staying out of that umbrella.
failureDesc:
'Oh dear... They're being spoiled...'

The tall fairy runs at us with jovial motions, holding the umbrella still.

It reveals a concealed arm stained with blood. It was running in a hurry, and the long arm is stretched toward us, trying to snatch us by the neck.

successDesc:
'Alright, then let's keep the festival going.'

The secret festival raged on in the field of battle where bullets rain.

After watching it for a while, you snuck out of the place, taking medical supplies dropped on the ground.

The sounds of bullets continued until you left.
failureDesc:
'Right, if you're gonna stay quiet...'

'Then it must mean you remember and will continue to remember.'

'Let me do you a favor.'

'The people you love will remember you as yet another victim that lost their head.'

This is still a battlefield;

The shooter loads its bullets with expertise.

successDesc:
The intravenous line was removed from its body.

When the red line was pulled out, its body regained vigor as if relieved of some kind of curse.

Perhaps the line was siphoning its nutrition rather than supplying it.
failureDesc:
The intravenous lines were too intricately intertwined to pull out.

While digging through the knots, you feel a sting on your hand.

You look down to find that a needle was poking into the back of your hand.

You quickly pulled it out, but you felt dizzy for some reason.

successDesc:
'It will be the newest patient of our hospital.'

'This is a small garden and haven.'

'They will be on good terms with the other patients.'

'It won't hurt; the anesthetics are plenty.'

We suffered many wounds on the way here, big and small.

Whether it's the body or the mind, wounds need to be treated in time.

If it's allowed to develop into a scar, it will not go away for certain.
failureDesc:
Even the most skilled doctor can't turn every operation into a success.

So it's natural for failure to be present even with multiple of them.

What can we do about it?

We'll have to bear the pain and limp our way back, as we always have.

successDesc:
The flask on the back fills with red.

Sated by the weight of the blood, the head lifts from the floor and points to the ceiling again.
failureDesc:
Alas, more blood was spilled than expected.

You fall to the floor as if you became the hammer.

Sated by the weight of the blood, the head lifts from the floor and points to the ceiling again.

successDesc:
Trying to lightly remove it is like brushing off dust

Is an affront to the greed that refused to dissipate even after shoving the head into it.

There was no choice but to watch as the hand reached out.
failureDesc:
We thought we pulled it out, but couldn't move forward.

Seeing it grasp at coattails until the end, you're sapped of strength, bound by the dreadful tenacity.

successDesc:
To hug is to embrace another.

The fingers that were stretched to grasp things clasp with another's appendages for the first time.

Between the quietly laced appendages, there was a warmth of blood beginning to circulate.

Like that, the rigid greed loosens.
failureDesc:
It seems the basic manners of clearing sweat from the hand before extending it for a handshake were neglected.

Your hand slipped, and the emptied palm wriggles as if to seek another left hand to grasp.

However, it no longer tries to recklessly grab things, seemingly fulfilled by the attempt.

successDesc:
We carefully walked into the dense fog.
Eerie howls surround us.

It was no longer possible to tell which way was forward.
All we could do was keep walking.

The fog cleared at one point,
and a trinket of endless ashen flow awaited us.

We then realized it wasn’t fog we walked through.
failureDesc:
We carefully walked into the dense fog.
Eerie howls surround us.

It was no longer possible to tell which way was forward.
All we could do was keep walking.
After a while, even advancing began to feel terrifying.

The fog grows thicker.
It feels as though you will no longer be able to escape.

successDesc:
You stared into the eye-like things.
Your piercing gaze met the song
of endless despair and wailing sockets.

You then realized that it wasn’t fog you were facing.
What you thought were its eyes and mouth
all turned out to be tricks played by shadows.

When you figured out that its howl was merely whistling wind,
all of it vanished.
failureDesc:
You stared into the eye-like things.
It returned the gaze, singing endless despair.

Just as you felt that the figure seemed to have grown,
we were engulfed by the fog.

successDesc:
With each rope that’s untied, the wail grows louder.

You feel sympathy for the trapped being.

Undoing the ropes, you begin to weep
along with their wailing.

One undone knot accompanies sorrow.
One undone knot accompanies lamentation.

When the last is released,
you discover that there is no one inside.

Perplexed, you examine the interior,
and find a single, red-jeweled beetle.
failureDesc:
With each rope that’s untied, the wail grows louder.

There’s more than one individual occupying the coffin.
Several people are clinging to each other, all crying.

The coffin bursts open, letting them out.

90200502.behaveDesc: Let’s just go back. Who should lead the way?
successDesc:
The red arms wave in the air,
as if pleading not to be left behind.

But, after another look,
it also seems as if they’re thankful for being left as they are.

Though the wailing hasn’t ceased,
the weeping seems to have softened, just a little.
failureDesc:
You disregard the chilling sensation and move on.

However, that feeling would linger,
even after a while of walking.

At one point, you stopped and turned around
to find a hand.

It was attached to an unnaturally scrawny red arm.

It grabbed its speechless victim.
A familiar note was added to the coffin’s wailing.

successDesc:
“You kent that?”

The paper flapped in disbelief.

“Well, if you really insist, knowing what’s coming…”

It took a step back, dousing itself with coffee.
Just then, a paper soldier
suddenly rushed at you.

“You shouldn’t underestimate paper’s integrity.”

Before you could respond to the peculiar voice,
the soldier attacked you.
failureDesc:
“You sure you read it right?”

“With a body like that, you can’t fulfill the contract written here!”

The paper sprayed coffee on the floor,
spewing insults.

Before we could make any excuses,
we got kicked out.

successDesc:
“‘Preciate it. You made the job easier.”

The paper soldiers nearby
start fighting each other.

Sometime later, the defeated soldiers’ bodies are torn,
exploding into paper cranes.

One of the cranes crawls your way.

“Take good care of that piece.”

The satisfied sheet of paper drenched itself in coffee.
failureDesc:
“That your way of turning someone down?”

Annoyed, the paper crumpled.

“There are basics to proper conversation.
I don’t wish to speak with someone
who can’t respect those basics.”

Before we could make any excuses,
we got kicked out.

successDesc:
{0} tries to comfort the Abnormality by saying that it will pass next time.
I also quickly add that what really matters is the will to pass...
... and even if it fails again, there'll always be a different path.

'You share such kind words to a stranger.'
'Right. I'll try again, and I'll make it this time.'
'But even if I don't... should I continue to live on, I'm sure I'll stumble across another opportunity. A different path.'
'Thank you.'

Fluttering butterflies dance around us, forming a line of butterflies before following {0}'s shadow.
failureDesc:
{0} interrogates the Abnormality, suggesting that maybe it just didn't do enough to prepare for the interview.
I also quickly tried to add that what really matters is the will to pass...
... but the Abnormality is already casting a downward gaze, beaten down from {0}'s brutally rational advice.

'I'm not looking for an advice right now.'
'No, actually, I don't think your advice will be of any comfort to me, now or later.'

The streetlight dies, and shadow fills the space.
